answers: Clean code = readable, maintainable, and simple. Named well, does one thing, and is easy to understand.
Principles: meaningful names, small functions, DRY (don't repeat yourself), single responsibility, and minimal comments.
Tips: code should read like prose, functions should do one thing, avoid deep nesting, and refactor ruthlessly.
Clean code by Robert C. Martin — essential reading. Key takeaway: code is read 10x more than it's written.
